title: Gravimetric Determination of Selenium using N-Benzenesulphonylhydroxylamine as Reducing Agent
author: L. R. MALEWAR
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of  Chemistry, College of  Science, Raipur, 492 002 (M.P.) 

  Manuscript received 10 February 1977, accepted 13    July 1977  

 The method described here allows determination of  selenium by reduction of  selenius acid in  hydrochloric acid using N-benzenesulphonylhydrosylamine. The procedure is particularly useful when selenium has to be determined in presence of tellurium. The method is rapid and simpler than the longer and time-consuming conventional methods and gives results which compare in precision. Analysis of  synthetic samples containing selenium and tellurium using the separation method gave acceptable results except in the case of metals which form insoluble chlorides. The proposed procedure is accurate and relatively free form Interferences.
